eventName: MV Hondius Andes virus response and U.S. monitoring cluster

locations.name: Port of Granadilla, Tenerife
locations.description: Cruise ship arrived in Tenerife for response coordination, disembarkation, and repatriation.

locations.name: Ushuaia cruise departure context
locations.description: Southern Cone cruise-route context for the MV Hondius outbreak.

locations.name: UNMC/Nebraska Medicine National Quarantine Unit
locations.description: U.S. repatriation, quarantine, monitoring, and treatment location for passengers linked to MV Hondius. AP reported 18 passengers at the University of Nebraska Medical Center special unit, including two under CDC quarantine orders. These are location-specific response counts, not confirmed U.S. Andes-virus cases.
locations.countNotes: AP reports 18 MV Hondius passengers at UNMC, with two under formal CDC quarantine orders. Reuters reports 18 monitored/quarantined in Nebraska and Atlanta and a previously inconclusive U.S. passenger later testing negative. No confirmed U.S. Andes-virus cases or deaths are assigned to this location.

locations.name: Emory University Hospital
locations.description: Supplemental/local reports describe MV Hondius passengers monitored or treated in Atlanta.
locations.countNotes: Reuters and local reporting support U.S. monitoring/testing at Atlanta/Emory; this marker remains screening/monitoring only and is not a confirmed U.S. Andes-virus case.

locations.name: New Jersey resident monitoring
locations.description: State health department update for exposed New Jersey residents under monitoring after MV Hondius travel.

locations.name: North Carolina passenger monitoring
locations.description: North Carolina health officials reported one passenger evacuated to Nebraska for quarantine with no current North Carolina cases.

locations.name: New York resident reports
locations.description: Supplemental news reports described New York residents linked to the MV Hondius response; retained as supplemental until matched to a public-health source.

locations.name: RIVM / GGD monitoring area
locations.description: Dutch public-health monitoring and guidance related to MV Hondius passengers and contacts.

locations.name: France passenger monitoring
locations.description: Supplemental reports describe French passenger monitoring and one serious case after repatriation.

locations.name: Bichat Hospital, Paris
locations.description: Location-specific evidence reports one French passenger treated in intensive care at Bichat Hospital.

locations.name: Eindhoven repatriation quarantine
locations.description: AP reports repatriation flights arrived in Eindhoven and passengers or crew were placed into quarantine; no case count is assigned to this marker.

locations.name: Radboud University Medical Center, Nijmegen
locations.description: Location-specific AP and CIDRAP evidence reports a patient treated at Radboud UMC and 12 hospital staff placed in preventive quarantine.

locations.name: Argentina exposure investigation
locations.description: AP and CIDRAP report Argentina planned an exposure-source investigation around locations visited before boarding; no deaths are assigned to this investigation marker.

locations.name: Rotterdam / Europoort ship disinfection logistics
locations.description: RIVM reported MV Hondius arrived in an enclosed part of the port of Rotterdam on May 18 for phased disembarkation, health checks, cleaning, and disinfection. Twenty-seven people remained on board, described as crew members and medical personnel. This is travel-linked logistics/monitoring, not a confirmed case or death location.
locations.countNotes: RIVM reported 27 people still on board and phased controlled disembarkation for health checks and cleaning/disinfection. HuffPost Spain reported remaining people would be tested/quarantined after disembarkation. Counts are logistics/monitoring metadata, not cases.

locations.name: Victoria, British Columbia hospital assessment
locations.description: Victoria hospital assessment/isolation location for the Canadian MV Hondius patient and partner. PHAC confirmed one positive hantavirus sample and AP reported the traveling partner was confirmed negative. These are location-specific Canada counts and no deaths are assigned to this marker.
locations.countNotes: PHAC confirmed one Canadian sample positive on May 16; AP reports the confirmed patient and traveling partner are a Yukon couple in their 70s in a Victoria hospital, with the partner confirmed negative. Monitoring/quarantine counts describe hospital isolation/assessment, not extra cases.

locations.name: Vancouver Island isolation and monitoring
locations.description: Four Canadian MV Hondius passengers returned to British Columbia for isolation and monitoring. One patient was later confirmed positive and their traveling partner confirmed negative; the Victoria marker carries the confirmed/negative patient-level counts to avoid double-counting.
sourceNames: British Columbia Office of the Provincial Health Officer
locations.countNotes: B.C./PHAC/AP evidence supports four Canadian passengers isolated/monitored in British Columbia. The one confirmed patient is counted at Victoria only to avoid duplicate confirmed markers.

locations.name: Yukon passenger origin context
locations.description: Residence/origin context for the confirmed Canadian patient and partner reported by AP. This origin marker intentionally does not repeat the Victoria confirmed count.
locations.countNotes: Yukon is recorded as origin/residence context only; the same confirmed patient is counted at the Victoria hospital marker, not again here.

locations.name: United States exposure monitoring area
locations.description: U.S. national exposure-monitoring baseline for MV Hondius and related flight contacts. Reuters reported CDC said 41 people were monitored and no U.S. Andes-virus cases from the cruise outbreak were confirmed; the previously inconclusive U.S. passenger was later confirmed negative.
sourceNames: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
locations.countNotes: Reuters reported CDC said 41 people in the U.S. were monitored, including 18 in Nebraska and Atlanta; Reuters later reported the previously inconclusive U.S. passenger tested negative. This is monitoring/exposure response, not confirmed infection.

locations.name: Washington state MV Hondius monitoring
locations.description: Axios Seattle reported six Washington residents were being monitored after possible Andes-virus exposure linked to MV Hondius, with all six asymptomatic as of May 18 per Washington Department of Health. This is exposure monitoring, not confirmed infection.
locations.countNotes: Washington Department of Health count as reported by Axios Seattle: six Washington residents monitored, all asymptomatic.

locations.name: King County, Washington monitoring
locations.description: Axios Seattle reported four of the monitored Washington residents were from King County. One King County resident from the ship was monitored at the national quarantine center in Nebraska. This is monitoring/exposure response, not a confirmed case.
locations.countNotes: King County location-specific monitoring count reported by Axios Seattle; not a confirmed infection count.

locations.name: Hospital Central de la Defensa Gómez Ulla
locations.description: Madrid quarantine and hospital monitoring location for 14 Spanish MV Hondius evacuees. Supplemental May 25/26 reporting says a second Spanish evacuee tested positive while already isolated; authorities said this did not change the general-public risk level. Location-specific deaths remain zero.

countSources.notes: Reuters reported Spain's health ministry said a Spanish national quarantined at Madrid's Gómez Ulla hospital tested positive and was transferred to a high-level isolation unit. This is location-specific evidence and does not change U.S. confirmed case counts.

El País reported a second positive among Spanish evacuees already under quarantine at Gómez Ulla, described the newly detected case as asymptomatic, and noted Spanish health authorities said public risk was unchanged.

HuffPost Spain reported the new positive was detected during periodic checks while the person was already under clinical surveillance and isolation at Gómez Ulla; authorities said the general-public risk level was not changed.

People reported the patient was one of 14 Spanish nationals evacuated from MV Hondius, the second Spaniard to test positive, and that Spanish health authorities said the detection did not change general-public risk.

locations.countNotes: Second Spanish evacuee tested positive while already under quarantine in Madrid. Authorities said this does not change the general-public risk level. These are location-specific quarantine/testing counts; MV Hondius event-wide deaths are not assigned to Madrid.

countSnapshots.sourceName: European Centre for Disease Prevention and Control
countSnapshots.notes: Latest official ECDC daily count used for the canonical official event count.

countSnapshots.sourceName: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
countSnapshots.notes: CDC current situation page states no Andes virus cases have been confirmed in the United States as a result of this outbreak to date; this is a U.S.-scope count note, not a replacement for ECDC's global outbreak count.
Earlier WHO Disease Outbreak News count retained in history.

RIVM reported three symptomatic people in the Netherlands tested after aircraft contact; two results were negative and one was pending at publication. This is monitoring evidence, not a confirmed Netherlands case count.
Health-system update describing U.S. citizens at UNMC/Nebraska Medicine quarantine and treatment units.
State health department monitoring update; these are exposed residents, not confirmed New Jersey cases.
Supplemental local news report around Emory monitoring; not an official confirmed case count.
AP-syndicated passenger tracing report; supplemental evidence and not an official U.S. case count.
CBS Atlanta reported two people evaluated at Emory, including one symptomatic passenger who tested negative for Andes virus according to HHS; supplemental evidence only.
Supplemental report describing one French and one American positive passenger; not used as official count unless corroborated.
Reuters-syndicated evacuation report; used as supplemental travel/response evidence, not a case count.
Counts vary by source/update. CIDRAP reports the outbreak grew to 11 total cases, 9 lab-confirmed, and 3 deaths. Location-specific quarantine counts apply only to Radboud/Nijmegen hospital personnel.

Counts vary by source/update. AP correction states WHO says 9 confirmed and 2 suspected worldwide; AP remains supplemental_news unless corroborated by WHO/ECDC/CDC. Evacuated passengers and crew are metadata, not cases.

PHAC reported one of four high-risk people in quarantine and symptom monitoring was presumptively positive for Andes hantavirus, with national-lab confirmation pending; the symptomatic person, their spouse, and a third person were moved to hospital for assessment. This is Canada response evidence, not an event-wide global confirmed-count update.

countSnapshots.sourceName: British Columbia Office of the Provincial Health Officer
countSnapshots.notes: B.C. stated four Canadians arriving from MV Hondius would be screened, assessed, isolated for at least 21 days, and monitored daily by public-health teams.

NBC described a Canadian passenger hospitalized in Victoria after a positive/presumptive test following MV Hondius exposure. Hantamap keeps this as supplemental evidence and preserves the official pending-confirmation framing.

NBC-style reporting said CDC was monitoring at least 41 people across 16 U.S. states with no known U.S. cases; this is monitoring/exposure-response evidence, not a confirmed U.S. Andes-virus case count.

AP reported 11 MV Hondius-linked hantavirus cases, including three deaths and eight lab-confirmed cases. AP also reported 18 passengers at the UNMC special unit in Omaha, with two under CDC quarantine orders. This supplemental snapshot differs from WHO/ECDC/CIDRAP histories and does not overwrite official counts.

Reuters reported CDC statements that 41 people in the U.S. were being monitored, including 18 in Nebraska and Atlanta, with no confirmed U.S. Andes-virus cases; a previously mild-positive result was later described as inconclusive and pending retest.

Axios Seattle reported Washington Department of Health information that six Washington residents, including four from King County, were being monitored after MV Hondius-related exposure and remained asymptomatic as of May 18.

Reuters reported a WHO official said the U.S. confirmed a previously inconclusive passenger tested negative, lowering the global case count to 10 from 11. Reuters also repeated U.S. monitoring context: no confirmed U.S. cases, 41 people monitored, including 18 in Nebraska and Atlanta. This remains a supplemental snapshot and does not overwrite official WHO/CDC/ECDC history.

PHAC confirmed one Canadian sample positive for hantavirus on May 16 and AP reported the confirmed case partner was negative. This supports Canadian location-level confirmation and does not add U.S. confirmed cases or location-level deaths.

AP reported PHAC confirmed one of four Canadian passengers tested positive, the traveling partner tested negative, both were in a Victoria hospital, all four Canadian cruise passengers were in isolation, and the Canadian patient was the 10th ship-linked positive. Event-wide deaths remain separate from Canada location markers.

RIVM reported MV Hondius arrived in Rotterdam with 27 people still on board for controlled disembarkation, health checks, cleaning, and disinfection. These are logistics/monitoring details, not confirmed cases.
People cited ECDC data indicating 11 confirmed and two probable MV Hondius-linked cases. This is a supplemental event-wide snapshot and does not assign deaths to Madrid or other child locations.
The Sun reported WHO-linked context of 12 suspected and confirmed cases and three deaths. Treated as low-confidence supplemental event-wide history unless matched to an official WHO update.

summary: Cruise-linked Andes virus outbreak response with official event-level counts, U.S. monitoring kept separate from confirmed cases, and a May 25/26 supplemental Madrid update for a second Spanish evacuee who tested positive while already quarantined at Gómez Ulla.

locations.name: Quad-County region
locations.description: Local public-health advisory region for a confirmed hantavirus case.

countSnapshots.sourceName: Carson City Health and Human Services via Carson Now
countSnapshots.notes: Local health officials confirmed a single hantavirus case in the Quad-County region.
Supplemental local news report referencing Quad-County officials.

summary: Local public-health-backed Nevada event for a single confirmed hantavirus case in the Quad-County region. This is unrelated to the MV Hondius Andes virus cluster.

locations.name: United States NNDSS surveillance area
locations.description: CDC weekly notifiable disease surveillance context for Hantavirus Pulmonary Syndrome.

countSnapshots.notes: Official surveillance source is tracked, but Hantamap does not infer a case count until a table extraction is verified.

summary: Official U.S. surveillance context for 2026 Hantavirus Pulmonary Syndrome. Counts remain null until table extraction is verified to avoid overstating surveillance data.

eventName: Douglas County Colorado fatal Sin Nombre hantavirus case

locations.name: Douglas County, Colorado
locations.description: Douglas County and CDPHE confirmed an adult Douglas County resident contracted Sin Nombre hantavirus and died. This rodentborne local case is not linked to the MV Hondius Andes-virus outbreak.
locations.countNotes: Location-specific confirmed fatal Sin Nombre hantavirus case. This is a separate rodentborne Colorado event and not linked to MV Hondius.

countSnapshots.notes: Douglas County shared a CDPHE/Douglas County Health Department statement confirming a Douglas County adult hantavirus case resulting in death; preliminary evidence suggests local rodent exposure and the case is not linked to MV Hondius.
People reported the confirmed Douglas County fatal case and cited Douglas County/CDPHE. Supplemental evidence only; official local-health statement remains the primary source.
Supplemental news evidence about the Douglas County fatal hantavirus case. Official Douglas County/CDPHE statement remains the primary source for counts and classification.

summary: Separate Douglas County, Colorado fatal Sin Nombre hantavirus case. This rodentborne event is unrelated to the MV Hondius Andes-virus outbreak.
